title: bulbophyllum perreflexum
intro: Bulbophyllum perreflexum is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Madagascar, characterized by its unique, curved inflorescence and small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Bulbophyllum perreflexum are said to include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as protection against infections and oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are consumed orally or applied topically to address various health concerns.
